hero.sub: A multi-agent AI system for real-time regulatory compliance orchestration. Transforming how enterprises detect, interpret, and respond to regulatory change.

about.heading: Bridging the Gap Between Regulatory Change and Organisational Response
about.body1: The accelerating pace and growing complexity of global regulatory frameworks across banking, aviation, data privacy, and digital governance have outpaced traditional manual compliance processes. Enterprises continue to rely on labour-intensive interpretation of regulatory circulars and legal amendments, resulting in delayed policy updates, fragmented implementation, and increased audit exposure.
about.body2: RegIntel Agent is a software-based, multi-agent AI system designed to transform compliance from a reactive workflow into a proactive, autonomous decision-making process. The system continuously ingests regulatory updates from official sources, applies large language models to extract structured obligations, and leverages a compliance knowledge graph to map clauses to enterprise policies, procedures, and IT controls.
about.velocityDesc: Continuous real-time scanning of regulatory sources, detecting changes within hours of publication.
about.interpretationDesc: LLM-based extraction of obligations, prohibitions, permissions, and deadlines with 90%+ precision.
about.mappingDesc: Dynamic knowledge graph linking clauses to internal policies, controls, and responsible departments.
about.generationDesc: Automated draft policy revisions, compliance checklists, and risk-ranked remediation tasks.

features.heading: Six Integrated Modules, One Autonomous Platform
features.sub: Each module addresses a distinct dimension of the compliance challenge, working together as a closed-loop intelligence system.

monitoring.title: Continuous Regulatory Monitoring
monitoring.body: Web crawlers and RSS/API feed parsers continuously scan government portals, financial regulators, and official legal bulletins. Change-detection algorithms flag new or amended publications and route them to the extraction pipeline within hours.

extraction.title: LLM-Based Clause Extraction
extraction.body: Documents are pre-processed through tokenisation and section segmentation, then passed to a fine-tuned GPT-4 class or Legal-BERT model that identifies and classifies obligations, prohibitions, permissions, deadlines, and conditional requirements at 90%+ precision.

graphDb.title: Knowledge Graph Construction
graphDb.body: Extracted clauses are represented as structured nodes within a Neo4j graph database. Edges encode relationships between regulatory clauses, internal policy documents, process controls, responsible departments, and affected IT systems.

rag.title: RAG-Augmented Policy Mapping
rag.body: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation layer allows the LLM to query the knowledge graph during gap analysis, combining graph traversal and semantic vector search to identify policy coverage gaps and map obligations to responsible owners.

riskEngine.title: Multi-Criteria Risk Scoring
riskEngine.body: Each regulatory clause is scored across severity of non-compliance, breadth of organisational impact, deadline proximity, and enforcement likelihood. SHAP-based explanations surface the scoring rationale to compliance officers for transparency.

actions.title: Automated Action Generation
actions.body: High-priority clauses trigger the generation of first-draft compliance artefacts: policy revisions, control adjustments, and audit checklists. Every artefact includes a full traceability chain linking it back to the originating regulatory clause.

architecture.sub: Five orchestrated modules form a closed-loop compliance intelligence pipeline, from regulatory publication to executable policy update.

monitoring.label: Regulatory Monitoring
monitoring.sub: Web Crawlers, RSS Parsers, Change Detection, Kafka Queue

extraction.label: Clause Extraction
extraction.sub: Tokenisation, Fine-Tuned LLM (GPT-4 / Legal-BERT), Confidence Threshold

knowledgeGraph.label: Knowledge Graph
knowledgeGraph.sub: Neo4j, Policy Inventory, Enterprise IT Controls, RAG Layer

riskEngine.label: Risk Scoring Engine
riskEngine.sub: Multi-Criteria Model, SHAP Explainability, Prioritised Risk Vector

actions.label: Action Generation
actions.sub: LLM + RAG, RBAC Approval Gate, Policy Drafts, Audit Trail

pipelines.sub: Each pipeline has a distinct responsibility and exposes a clean API boundary, enabling modular development and independent scaling.

p1.title: Pipeline 1 — Ingestion
p1.desc: Automated ingestion system that continuously monitors regulatory sources, fetches newly published documents, deduplicates them, extracts structured clauses using LLM, generates base context, and stores the processed document in a structured format.
steps: Fetch new documents via RSS, API, or scraping
Text cleaning — remove HTML, headers, whitespace
Store structured document in MongoDB / PostgreSQL

endpoints.desc: Full document with clauses and context

p2.title: Pipeline 2 — RAG, Mapping & LLM Engine
p2.desc: Receives enriched documents from Pipeline 1 and applies a parallelised hybrid retrieval strategy combining graph traversal (Neo4j) and semantic vector search to map each clause to internal policies and detect compliance gaps.
steps: For each clause: trigger hybrid retrieval
Graph Retrieval: Clause → Control → Policy → Department
Semantic Retrieval: vector embedding similarity search
Context Builder merges and deduplicates candidates
LLM selects best-matching policy and detects gaps
Assign responsible department by policy ownership
Store mapping results, return enriched clauses

endpoints.desc: Full document with all clauses enriched with mapping data
Deep-dive for a single clause including retrieval sources

p3.title: Pipeline 3 — Risk Scoring & Action Generation
p3.desc: Evaluates mapped clauses from Pipeline 2, calculates weighted risk scores, assigns priority levels (Low / Medium / High), and generates actionable compliance recommendations using LLM grounded in the knowledge graph.
steps: Calculate severity score for each clause
Calculate impact across systems and operations
Calculate urgency based on deadline proximity
LLM generates policy updates, alerts, checklists
Store results, expose via dashboard API

endpoints.desc: Compute risk score and priority for a document

team.sub: Third-year undergraduate students from RV College of Engineering, Bengaluru, developing the RegIntel Agent for the Unisys Innovation Program 2026.

footer.desc: A multi-agent AI system developed at RV College of Engineering, Bengaluru, for the Unisys Innovation Program 2026.
